title: lichen_l2.h/lora_l2.h: MTU constant duplicated with comment explaining why
description:
Locations: lichen_l2.h:49, lora_l2.h:57

LICHEN_L2_MTU (200) is defined in lichen_l2.h with a comment: 'Must match LICHEN_LORA_MTU in lora_l2.h. Duplication note: We duplicate rather than include lora_l2.h to avoid forcing consumers of lichen_l2.h to pull in LoRa driver types.'

lora_l2.h computes LICHEN_LORA_MTU as (255 - 55) = 200.

What goes wrong: The values currently match, but nothing enforces this. If someone changes LICHEN_LORA_FRAME_OVERHEAD, the mismatch will cause silent packet truncation or rejection. The 'must match' comment is documentation, not enforcement.

Suggested fix: In lichen_l2.c, add: BUILD_ASSERT(LICHEN_L2_MTU == LICHEN_LORA_MTU, 'MTU mismatch between L2 layers'). This file already includes both headers.
close_reason: Fixed: BUILD_ASSERT already added to lichen_l2.c (lines 25-34) enforcing LICHEN_L2_MTU == LICHEN_LORA_MTU at compile time. If the values ever diverge, compilation will fail with clear error message.
